title: Articles
intro: Articles go before a noun to say 'the' (definite) or 'a/some' (indefinite). In Spanish they must match the gender and number of the noun.

sections.title: Two handy contractions
sections.content:
When 'de' (of) or 'a' (to) come before 'el', they merge:
• de + el = del → Vengo del mercado. (I come from the market.)
• a + el = al → Voy al banco. (I'm going to the bank.)
This ONLY happens with 'el' — never with 'la', 'los', or 'las'.

questions.explanation: silla is feminine singular → una silla ✓
a + el = al (contraction). Never write 'a el' separately! ✓

questions.prompt: Las flores son ___ flores del jardín.
questions.explanation: flores is feminine plural → las flores ✓

questions.prompt: Tengo ___ hermano y ___ hermana. Which pair is correct?
questions.explanation: We're talking about having 'a' brother and 'a' sister → un hermano / una hermana ✓
de + el = del (contraction). supermercado is masculine → del ✓
de + el = del. This is a fixed rule in Spanish! ✓
